mappings.rationaleAI: The Aztecs' Triple Alliance, Nahuatl language, chinampas agriculture, tribute system, polytheistic religion, human sacrifice, and Spanish conquest directly address the development of the Aztec state (C.2) and Aztec culture and society up to the Spanish conquest (C.3).

Mesoamerica's coverage of Olmec foundations, Teotihuacan, Maya and Zapotec writing systems, vigesimal mathematics, calendrical systems, and the Aztec Triple Alliance provides comprehensive context across all major outline sections.

Maya civilization from Preclassic origins to Classic city-states and Postclassic resurgence addresses pre-Maya villages (B.1.c), lowland Maya (B.1.d), Tzakol and Tepeu cultures (B.2.a.ii), Late Classic lowland Maya including collapse (B.2.c), and Toltec-Maya culture at Chichen-Itza (C.1).
Mexico's pre-Columbian civilizations (Maya, Aztec/Mexica) and Spanish conquest provide context for the character of Meso-American civilization (A) and the Post-Classic period (C).
The pre-Columbian era's coverage of Mesoamerican civilizations (Olmec, Maya, Aztec) provides comprehensive context across all outline sections.
Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire directly relates to Aztec culture and society up to the time of the Spanish conquest (C.3).
The Spanish Empire's conquest of the Aztec Empire and Habsburg expansion directly addresses the Spanish conquest context of Aztec civilization (C.3).
North American history's pre-Columbian Indigenous civilizations provide context for the character and achievements of Meso-American civilization (A).
The post-classical period's Americas coverage provides context for the Classic period (B.2) and the Post-Classic period's Toltec and Aztec developments (C).
